title: Replace Third-Party CSV Import with DataTables Built-In Import
story: As a user importing metrics from CSV, I want a built-in import experience that supports all metric fields, so that I don't depend on a paid third-party library.
description:
Replace the paid third-party CSV import library with DataTables' native import extension. The new import should support column mapping and all recently added metric fields.

### New Import Fields
- Description
- Logic
- Date Name Field
- Business Owner
- Metric Target

### Import Flow
1. Click "Import from Excel / CSV"
2. Upload file screen appears
3. Map CSV columns to metric fields
4. Click Import to save

### Reference
[DataTables Import Extension](https://editor.datatables.net/examples/extensions/import.html)
acceptance_criteria: CSV import uses DataTables built-in import instead of third-party library
Import supports column mapping between CSV and metric fields
All new fields are importable: Description, Logic, Date Name Field, Business Owner, Metric Target
Import UX provides file upload, column mapping, and import confirmation
ux_changes: CSV import flow redesigned with file upload screen, column mapping interface, and import button. Replaces the previous third-party import UI.
middleware_changes: Backend import handler updated to accept data from the DataTables import extension and map columns to metric fields including the five new fields.
implementation_notes: Initial implementation on local showed the DataTables import had a less polished UX than the third-party library. Developer first added the new fields to the existing third-party import, then later replaced it entirely with the DataTables import which was refined to match the existing UX quality.
comment_summary: Developer first implemented the DataTables import locally but noted the UX was less attractive than the existing library. After discussion, new fields were added to the third-party import as an interim step. Later, the DataTables import was fully completed with file upload, column mapping, and import — submitted for review on development.
